revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:氷上姉子神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hikami Anego Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Hikami Anego Jinja Haiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Midori-ku, Nagoya|en|Midori-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q1155209|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}, Ōdaka-chō Hikamiyama 1-3 |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|3|41.26|N|136|55|48.95|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Hikami Anego Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} |Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, {{ill|External branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Sessha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/External branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} |Foundation = (Tradition) 4th year of Emperor Chūai's reign |Main_hall = |Festival = First Sunday in October (Ōdaka Festival) |Map = {{location map+|Japan Aichi|width=220|float=|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=7|lat_sec=38.59|lon_deg=136|lon_min=54|lon_sec=31.22|label={{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}|position=top|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{location map~|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=3|lat_sec=41.26|lon_deg=136|lon_min=55|lon_sec=48.95|label='''Hikami Anego Shrine'''|position=bottom|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }}}} [[File:Hikami-Anego-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Tumulus and ancient shrines distribution map of South Nagoya during the tumulus period.png|thumb|380x380px|{{center|Distribution map of ancient shrines and tumuli around Nagoya during the Kofun period}}]] [[File:Hikami-Yama-no-zu.png|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Reproduction of "Map of Hikami Mountain"}}]] [[File:Hikami-Anego-jinja Motomiya-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Former shrine (left) and monument to the residence of Miyazu-hime (right)}}]] '''Hikami Anego Shrine''' (氷上姉子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Midori-ku, Nagoya|en|Midori-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q1155209|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its historical Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Currently, it serves as an external branch shrine of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. The shrine is situated on a hill known as "Hikamiyama" (氷上山/火上山) and is said to be the site where the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|lt=Kusanagi no Tsurugi|lt=Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi|ja|草薙剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天叢雲剣}}, one of the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, was enshrined before the founding of Atsuta Shrine. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "O-Hikami-san." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following deity:Shrine history pamphlet. * {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} (Miyasu-hime/Miyazu-hime) The deity is associated with the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari clan|1=Owari clan}}, as indicated in texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, and fragments of the {{ill|Fudoki|en|Fudoki|qid=Q1194534|lt=Fudoki|1=Fudoki}}. The texts recount how {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} wed Miyazu-hime and entrusted her with the Kusanagi sword. == History == === Foundation === According to tradition recorded in the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine's origin myths|ja|熱田神宮縁起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine's origin myths|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578841|1=Atsuta Shrine's origin myths}}, Hikami Anego Shrine originated as the residence of Miyazu-hime, who safeguarded the Kusanagi sword before the establishment of Atsuta Shrine. It is said to have been founded during the reign of Emperor Chūai and relocated to its current site during the reign of Empress Jitō. === Chronological Events === The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 AD) under the name "Hi-no-Kami Anego Shrine." Over centuries, the shrine was maintained and revered as a significant religious site connected to the Owari clan and Yamato Takeru legends. == Architecture == The current main hall (honden) was relocated in 1893 from the Eight Swords Shrine, a subordinate shrine of Atsuta Shrine. Additional reconstruction and repairs were undertaken in 1986. File:Hikami-Anego-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Odaka Saiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|Ōdaka Rice Fields (dedicated sacred fields of Atsuta Shrine) File:Hikami-Anego-jinja Kutsunugijima-ato.JPG|Kutsunugi-jima remains == Events == The shrine hosts several annual rituals: * '''Grand Kagura Performance (March)''' - A traditional dance said to have originated at Atsuta Shrine. * '''Ōdaka Festival (October)''' - Features parades with decorative floats and is a major local celebration. == Access == * Address: Ōdaka-chō Hikamiyama 1-3, Midori-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture * '''Transportation:''' ** 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ōdaka Station|en|Ōdaka Station|qid=Q871424|lt=Ōdaka Station|1=Ōdaka Station}} on the JR Tōkaidō Line. ** 25-minute walk from {{ill|Nawa Station|en|Nawa Station|qid=Q845442|lt=Nawa Station (Aichi)|1=Nawa Station}} on the Meitetsu Tokoname Line. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/jingu/about/yukari/yukari_detail03.html Official page] - {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}} }} {{translated page|ja|氷上姉子神社|version=101853889|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Atsuta Faith}} [[ja:氷上姉子神社]] {{Commons category|Hikami-Anego-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11549548}} [[Category:Atsuta Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Midori-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Midori-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
